key_insight: MISTnetX effectively bridges atomistic molecular dynamics simulations with continuum finite-element models to predict the shock-to-detonation transition in energetic materials.
engineering_relevance: This research offers a novel approach for accurately modeling complex microstructure interactions that influence energetic material behavior under shock conditions.
method: Developed a convolutional deep neural network (MISTnetX) trained on MD simulations.
Captured complex shock-microstructure interactions and hotspot formations.
Provided sub-grid information to enhance FE simulations across multiple physics domains.
evidence_signal: MISTnetX was validated using synthetic data from nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composites.
watch_item: The effectiveness of MISTnetX in real-world applications needs careful validation beyond simulated environments.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
